name: Monstera 'Burle Marx Flame'

morphology.leaf_shape: Elongated, narrow, flame-like leaves with dramatic, deep internal fenestrations and thick lobes resembling ribs
morphology.leaf_ratio: Distinctly longer than wide, approximately 3:1 to 4:1 length-to-width ratio
morphology.texture: Smooth, glossy, and firm (coriaceous) leaf substance
morphology.petiole: Green, relatively slender, cylindrical, and sturdy
morphology.growth_habit: Climbing hemiepiphyte with a graceful, sculptural habit, producing thick aerial roots from nodes
morphology.mature_size: Overall leaf length 40–70 cm; petioles 30–50 cm; mature height up to 1.5 meters when supported

critical_traits: Elongated, narrow leaves with deep, flame-like lobing and internal fenestrations resembling ribs
Solid deep green foliage with a smooth, glossy texture and no variegation
Slender green petioles without wings or sheath wrapping
Thick, stubby aerial roots emerging from the nodes
Graceful, upright climbing growth habit requiring vertical support
negative_traits: Variegated foliage (no mint, white, yellow sectoral or marbled patterns)
Entire leaf margins without any fenestrations or lobes
Perfectly heart-shaped (cordate) leaves with no holes
Velvety, matte, or hairy (pubescent) leaf surfaces
habitat: Cultivated tropical climbing aroid; best grown in warm, humid understory-like conditions with bright indirect light and vertical support. Ecologically consistent with rainforest hemiepiphyte Monstera species.
origin: Horticultural cultivar associated with Brazilian aroid cultivation; exact original selection history is debated in cultivation.
